findingsNew.title: Root README delegates the /goal plugin contract to an entrypoint inventory that does not define that contract
findingsNew.claim: The public root README sends operators to .opencode/plugins/README.md for the /goal plugin contract, but that document does not contain the mk-goal config/contract surface needed for the state, injection, lifecycle and autonomy claims the root README is describing.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked whether .opencode/plugins/README.md had a mk-goal config/contract subsection, a link to the goal contract, or goal env/output/archive behavior beyond the entrypoint row; none was present in the reviewed ranges.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: If the root README intended only the low-level OpenCode loader/export contract, .opencode/plugins/README.md:26-28 and .opencode/plugins/README.md:49 partially satisfy that. The surrounding root bullets discuss /goal state, injection and autonomy, so the operator-facing goal contract is the more likely intended target.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if the root README wording is narrowed to entrypoint loader contract or if .opencode/plugins/README.md gains a first-class mk-goal contract subsection linked from the root pointer.

findingsNew.title: Skill Advisor README contradicts the feature catalog's verified /goal live-tool status
findingsNew.claim: The system-skill-advisor README says /goal live OpenCode-run tool invocation remains under investigation, while the sibling feature catalog says a live opencode serve run verified tool listing and live model state persistence.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked the feature catalog for whether it only documented unit/tool-path tests or an unverified plan; it explicitly claims opencode serve tool listing plus a live model turn persisting state.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The README may have intended to say active autonomous continuation invocation is still under investigation, not basic mk_goal / mk_goal_status tool invocation. If so, the wording is ambiguous and should be narrowed.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to a no-finding wording cleanup if the README is clarified to refer only to live autonomous continuation and the feature catalog keeps basic tool invocation verification separate.

findingDetails.title: Root README delegates the /goal plugin contract to an entrypoint inventory that does not define that contract
findingDetails.scopeProof: Cross-read the public root pointer, the plugin entrypoint document and the dedicated goal contract reference; this affects root docs and the plugin/operator docs rather than an isolated typo.

findingDetails.title: Skill Advisor README contradicts the feature catalog's verified /goal live-tool status
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact sibling-doc contradiction found between one README line and one feature catalog line; no broader same-class claim was made.

priorFindings: P1-002 indirectly strengthened but not duplicated

title: Root README delegates the /goal plugin contract to an entrypoint inventory that does not define that contract
recommendedFix: Retarget README.md:1233 to .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/hooks/goal_plugin.md and optionally add a related-link breadcrumb from .opencode/plugins/README.md.

title: Skill Advisor README contradicts the feature catalog's verified /goal live-tool status
recommendedFix: Update the README note to match the verified feature catalog, or narrow it to say only active autonomous continuation live invocation remains under investigation.

name: plugins-readme-both-plugins-wording
summary: The entrypoint table lists six plugin files including mk-goal.js, but the configuration section still says Both plugins and documents only mk-skill-advisor and mk-code-graph.
The root pointer should likely target goal_plugin.md, but existing P1-002 means that reference still needs completion for env/output coverage before it is fully authoritative.
